content:
# Romans Chapter 11 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/ROM_011_page_001.jpg

1. I say then, did the God reject the people of him? May it never be! For I also am an Israelite, from [the] seed of Abraham, of [the] tribe of Benjamin.

2. The God did not reject the people of him whom he foreknew. Or do you not know what the writing says in [the passage about] Elias, how he appeals to the God against Israel?

3. "Lord, the prophets of you they killed, the altars of you they tore down, and I alone am left, and they seek the life of me."

4. But what does the divine response say to him? "I have reserved for myself seven thousand men who did not bend [the] knee to the Baal."

5. So then also in the present time a remnant according to [the] election of grace has come to be.

6. But if by grace, [it is] no longer from works; otherwise the grace is no longer grace.

7. What then? What Israel is seeking, this it did not obtain; but the election obtained [it], and the rest were hardened,

8. just as it has been written, "The God gave to them a spirit of stupor, eyes not to see and ears not to hear, until this very day."

9. And David says, "Let the table of them become a snare and a trap and a stumbling block and a retribution to them;

10. let the eyes of them be darkened not to see, and the back of them bend always."

11. I say then, did they stumble that they might fall? May it never be! But by the trespass of them [came] the salvation to the nations, to provoke them to jealousy.

12. And if the trespass of them [is the] riches of [the] world, and the loss of them [is the] riches of [the] nations, how much more the fullness of them?

13. And to you I speak, the nations. Inasmuch as I am an apostle of [the] nations, I glorify the ministry of me,

14. if somehow I might provoke to jealousy the flesh of me and save some of them.

15. For if the rejection of them [is the] reconciliation of [the] world, what [will] the acceptance [be] except life from [the] dead?

16. And if the firstfruit [is] holy, also the lump; and if the root [is] holy, also the branches.

17. But if some of the branches were broken off, and you, being a wild olive, were grafted in among them and became a partaker of the root of the fatness of the olive tree,

18. do not boast against the branches. But if you boast — you do not bear the root, but the root [bears] you.

19. You will say then, "Branches were broken off that I might be grafted in."

20. Good [point]. By the unbelief they were broken off, and you stand by the faith. Do not be high-minded, but fear.

21. For if the God did not spare the natural branches, neither will he spare you.

22. See therefore [the] kindness and [the] severity of God: upon the [ones] having fallen, severity; but upon you, [the] kindness of God, if you remain in the kindness; otherwise you also will be cut off.

23. And those also, if they do not remain in the unbelief, will be grafted in; for the God is able to graft them in again.

24. For if you were cut from the wild olive tree according to nature, and against nature were grafted into [the] cultivated olive tree, how much more will these, the [ones] according to nature, be grafted into the own olive tree of them?

25. For I do not want you to be ignorant, brothers, of this mystery — that you may not be wise in yourselves — that a hardening from a part has come upon Israel, until the fullness of the nations comes in,

26. and thus all Israel will be saved, just as it has been written, "The deliverer will come from Sion; he will turn away ungodliness from Iakob.

27. And this [is] the covenant of me with them, when I take away the sins of them."

28. According to the good news, [they are] enemies for your sake; but according to the election, [they are] beloved because of the fathers.

29. For the gifts and the calling of the God [are] irrevocable.

30. For just as you once disobeyed the God, but now were shown mercy by the disobedience of these,

31. so also these now disobeyed, by the mercy of you, that they also may now be shown mercy.

32. For the God shut up all into disobedience, that he might have mercy on all.

33. O depth of riches and wisdom and knowledge of God! How unsearchable [are] the judgments of him, and untraceable the ways of him!

34. "For who knew [the] mind of [the] Lord? Or who became counselor of him?"

35. "Or who gave first to him, and it will be repaid to him?"

36. Because from him and through him and to him [are] all things. To him [be] the glory into the ages. Amen.

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **v.6 — Sinaiticus shorter reading:** Sinaiticus omits the second half of the verse found in later manuscripts: "But if of works, it is no longer grace; otherwise work is no longer work." The shorter reading is likely original.

2. **v.25 — "This mystery" (τὸ μυστήριον τοῦτο):** In Paul, μυστήριον is not "mysterious" but "a previously hidden divine plan now revealed." The mystery: Israel's hardening is partial and temporary; it serves the inclusion of the Gentiles; "all Israel" will ultimately be saved.

3. **v.26 — "All Israel will be saved":** The meaning of "all Israel" is debated: (a) The whole ethnic nation; (b) The elect within Israel across history; (c) The full number of Jews who will believe. Paul's context favors a corporate reading — Israel as a whole people, not necessarily every individual.

4. **v.29 — "Irrevocable" (ἀμεταμέλητα):** Literally "not-to-be-repented-of." God does not take back his gifts or his calling. Israel's election stands despite their current unbelief. This is the theological foundation for Paul's hope.

5. **vv.33-36 — The doxology:** Echoes Isaiah 40:13 and Job 41:11. Paul ends the Israel section not with a theological solution but with worship. The depth of God's riches, wisdom, and knowledge transcends human comprehension. Some things are not problems to be solved but mysteries to be worshipped.

— EMET, AI Translator
